pom.xml.general_idea: Update the project's target Java version property from 17 to 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project must target JDK 21 to utilize new language features and ensure compatibility with the target runtime environment.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the JaCoCo Maven plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of JaCoCo may fail to instrument or analyze classes compiled for Java 21 due to bytecode version incompatibilities.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Checkstyle Maven plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of Checkstyle may not support parsing or analyzing Java 21 syntax or bytecode.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Maven Compiler Plugin to a version that supports the 'release' flag and Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of the compiler plugin may not fully support the 'release' flag or Java 21 specific compilation features.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Maven WAR Plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of the WAR plugin may encounter issues packaging applications targeting Java 21.

pom.xml.general_idea: Set the scope of jakarta.annotation-api to 'provided' to avoid conflicts with the container-provided API.
pom.xml.why_exists: Including the API in the application jar can cause classloading conflicts or version mismatches with the Jakarta EE container at runtime.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated 'source' and 'target' compiler configuration with the modern 'release' flag.
pom.xml.why_exists: The 'source' and 'target' flags are deprecated in favor of 'release' which ensures consistent API usage and bytecode versioning for the target JDK.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add the 'release' flag to the test compiler configuration to ensure tests are compiled for Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without the 'release' flag, tests might be compiled with incompatible source/target settings or default to an older JDK version.

Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() to comply with Java 21 API changes.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: List.get(int) is deprecated for removal in Java 21; using it may cause compile-time deprecation warnings or errors depending on compiler settings, and List.getFirst() is the required replacement.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Compilation error: 'serialVersionUID' is not accessible from module java.base (or similar module access error) because the field is in a different module than java.io.Serializable and lacks the @Serial annotation required since Java 14.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 17+ enforces that serialVersionUID fields in serializable classes must be annotated with @Serial (or be effectively final and private static final long) to avoid compile-time errors or warnings treated as errors in strict builds.
